LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 128.7.MATTER CALLED, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT CONTESTED. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CCP 128.7 IS GRANTED IN SMALL PART. PLAINTIFF REGIONAL PROTECTION AGENCY, INC.'S FIRST, EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, QUANTUM MERUIT AND PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL ARE STRICKEN. ALL OTHER MONETARY AND NON-MONETARY SANCTIONS SOUGHT BY UBER ARE DENIED. RPA'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS ALSO DENIED.(SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR RPA IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC.3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TOREEANOM@AOL.COM =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS AND PRAYERS FROM THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT AND INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF REGIONAL PROTECTION AGENCY, INC. FILING A MOTION TO AMEND TO SEEK PUNITIVE DAMAGES AS TO THE INTERFERENCE CLAIM IF AND WHEN IT OBTAINS FACTS ALLOWING IT TO DO SO. AN IMPLIED COVENANT CLAIM IS A CONTRACT CLAIM FOR WHICH PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE NOT AVAILABLE. RPA HAS NOT ALLEGED SUFFICIENT ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING CORPORATE LIABILITY FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST UBER ON ITS INTERFERENCE CLAIM NOR DOES IT APPEAR THAT IT IS NOW ABLE TO DO SO. HOWEVER, AFTER DISCOVERY AND FACTUAL INVESTIGATION, IT MAY BE ABLE TO DO SO AND IS NOT PRECLUDED FROM FILING A MOTION TO AMEND TO DO SO IF IT OBTAINS SUFFICIENT SUPPORTING FACTS. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT and Cross-Complainant UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE Portions of Plaintiff's Verified 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT for Damages.

Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations and prayers from the second and third causes of action for breach of the implied covenant and interference with contractual relations in the first amended complaint is granted without prejudice to plaintiff Regional Protection Agency, Inc. filing a motion to amend to seek punitive damages as to the interference claim if and when it obtains facts allowing it to do so. An implied covenant claim is a contract claim for which punitive damages are not available. RPA has not alleged sufficient ultimate facts supporting corporate liability for punitive damages against Uber on its interference claim nor does it appear that it is now able to do so. However, after discovery and factual investigation, it may be able to do so and is not precluded from filing a motion to amend to do so if it obtains sufficient supporting facts.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 128.7
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Elizabeth Lee, Esq. of Shook Hardy & Bacon, 415.544.1900, appeared for Defendant Uber;
Angela Johnson, Esq. (in-house counsel), appearing for Defendant Uber;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 128.7.

Matter called, the tentative ruling is not contested. The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion for sanctions pursuant to CCP 128.7 is granted in small part. Plaintiff Regional Protection Agency, Inc.'s first, eighth and ninth causes of action for breach of contract, quantum meruit and promissory estoppel are stricken. All other monetary and non-monetary sanctions sought by Uber are denied. RPA's request for monetary sanctions is also denied. This motion should never have been brought and is arguably an abuse of the 128.7 procedure notwithstanding that Uber is correct that RPA should have dismissed its claims seeking payment for services once RPA acknowledged that it had received full payment for services. The portions of Uber's motion seeking more than $100,000 in sanctions and the striking of the second through seventh causes of action has a strong flavor of a bullying tactic by a large company with huge resources against a litigation adversary with substantially less resources, wholly apart from the procedural and substantive lack of merit to those portions of the motion. Uber's failure to serve RPA with the declaration of Michael Zito at least 21 days before filing this motion precludes Uber from obtaining any monetary sanctions. Uber's request for an order striking the second through seventh causes of action grounds (i.e., its payment of all monies due for services and its failure to receive the employment contracts) does not take into account that those claims allege misconduct by Uber other than failure to pay monies for services and interference with contract (such as misuse of proprietary information) and that some or all of those claims can succeed even if there were no written contracts and even if (contrary to RPA's allegation) the contracts were at will contracts. Because RPA should have dismissed the first, eighth and ninth causes of action after being served with 21 day notice of Uber's intent to file this motion and its counsel should have been more candid about the nature of the employment contracts, RPA's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Counsel for RPA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with CRC. 3.1312. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

Defendant U.S. Security Associates, Inc.'s motion to strike the prayer for punitive damages in the complaint filed by plaintiff Regional Protection Agency, Inc. is granted with twenty days leave to amend. Regional Protection clarified that it is only seeking punitive damages against US Security on the third cause of action for interference with contractual relations. The third cause of action fails to allege sufficient ultimate facts to support punitive damages. If it can do so in good faith, Regional Protection is granted leave to allege facts showing that one or more officers, directors, or managing agents of US Security acted with malice, fraud or oppression in interfering with Regional Protection's employment contracts.
